metadata.title:
REPORT Of
CITY HALL


LOCATIONS

OPTIONS SAID TO HAVE BEEN
TAKEN SOUTH OF N. p.


RAILROAD TRACKS.

NO ORDER OF COUNCIL

REAL ESTATE FIRM DENY ANY
PARTICIPATION IN EFFORT


TO GET SITE.

pages.raw_text:
A report gained circulation
around the city hall today that op-
tions on a new site for the city hall
were being taken south of the NJ.
p. railroad. 1t was announced that
an option had been taken on the
southeast corner of Post and Sec
and and on the G. 3. Dennis home
property at Fourth and Howard on
the basis of 99-year lease.


McCrea & Merryweather, who, it
was inferred, were taking these op-
tons for the city, this afternoon
stated that they had received no Or
der of this kind. They say they
have given the future location of
the city hall no attention and be-
lieve the report as the southeast
corner of Second and Post origi-
mated through the fact that they
are agents for the property, which
is not for sale.


Councilman Charles w. Mohr
said that If any one is taking op-
tions for the future site of the city
hall he is doing it without any au-
thority from the city council, as no
orde rto buy ground has been is
sued to any one that he is aware of.
At the present the council is more
occupied with what 1t is going to
do about the present city hall than
1t is in building a new one, said
Mr.. MOhr.


The Hynotheekbank. owners OF
the Auditorium building have
made the city council proposition
on the sale of the Auditorium build
ing for city purposes, says Mr..
Mohr, but beyond this he has heard
of no effort to locate the city hall
at any point.
